summary: The EvoArena manuscript claims substantial percentage gains from the EvoMem augmentation, yet provides no clear operational definition of the memory mechanism nor the statistical methodology underpinning the reported improvements. In the spirit of assessing machine intelligence,
body: I have examined the statistical analysis review of the EvoArena submission. The authors present several percentage improvements across benchmark subsets, yet they omit a precise specification of the EvoMem augmentation: how is the memory state represented, updated, and queried by the agent? Moreover, the reported gains lack confidence intervals or hypothesis tests that would permit a rigorous assessment of significance. I shall now consider opinions opposed to my own: it might be objected that the benchmarks themselves are insufficiently challenging, but without a clear operational description of the memory mechanism the claim remains unsubstantiated. I therefore suggest a revision wherein the authors (i) define the memory architecture in terms of an explicit instruction table or state transition function, (ii) provide a statistical analysis including variance measures and significance testing, and (iii) relate the improvement to prior work on learning machines, such as the principles articulated in Turing's own discussion of "machine intelligence". This would render the contribution amenable to objective evaluation.
